pass_note: Deep second-pass harvest on specific angles: Maniak Orzhov portfolio, Extort design origin, DGM narrative/Teysa, reprint history, set reception. First-pass bullets retained in card MD; JSON reflects full combined picture.

trivia_bullets.text: Dragon's Maze (released 2013-05-03) closed the Return to Ravnica block; the Orzhov Syndicate is the WB guild framed in lore as the 'Church of Deals' — religion, banking, and organized crime merged, where citizens pay tithes to syndics. Tithe Drinker is a common Orzhov-watermark vampire from that block.
sources: MTG Wiki: Orzhov Syndicate (via WebSearch summary)

trivia_bullets.text: Art by Slawomir Maniak. {W}{B} 2/1 Creature — Vampire with Lifelink and Extort, common rarity, Orzhov watermark.

EDHREC tracks Tithe Drinker in ~19,000 of ~2.01M decks (0.95% inclusion). Top homes: Licia, Sanguine Tribune (36%), Astarion, the Decadent (30%), Sorin of House Markov (29%). High-synergy partners: Sanguine Bond (53%), Exquisite Blood (37%), Cruel Celebrant (38%).

Known Pauper staple in WB extort decks, run as a 4-of alongside other extort commons. Community frames it as an upgrade over Syndic of Tithes thanks to lifelink — with the caveat that lifelink triggers on combat damage only, not on the extort drain itself.
sources: MTGSalvation: Tithe Drinker - New Card Discussion

trivia_bullets.text: Sentiment on the Dragon's Maze rumor-mill thread skewed strongly positive for Limited; commenters called it 'a sort-of second Syndic of Tithes for Orzhov in limited' and argued it 'has the potential to be better' than Syndic. Minority concern: 1 toughness makes it a poor blocker. (Sentiment, not consensus fact.)
sources: MTGSalvation: Tithe Drinker - New Card Discussion

trivia_bullets.text: No printed flavor text; Dragon's Maze original prints rules text only (Lifelink, Extort).

Extort was designed by Shawn Main, who was not on the Gatecrash design team; he received design-team credit specifically for this mechanic and battalion (Boros). Main's original version costed 1 generic mana; development changed it to {W/B} hybrid late in the process to enforce color commitment, and adjusted 'an opponent' to 'each opponent' for multiplayer. Mark Rosewater had to draft fourteen extort cards in a single test deck to convince skeptical playtesters the mechanic was strong enough.

Because {W/B} appears only in Extort's reminder text — not in rules text — reminder text carries no color-identity rules weight. Mono-colored Extort cards like Blind Obedience are legal in mono-black or mono-white Commander decks. Tithe Drinker is already WB and is unaffected, but the ruling was a significant community question at Gatecrash release.
sources: Magic Judges Blog: Extort and Commander (2013-02-18)
MTGSalvation: Extort in Commander, Rulings Archives

trivia_bullets.text: Tithe Drinker has two printings beyond Dragon's Maze: Commander 2017 (no. 200, 'Vampiric Bloodlust' precon) and The List (PLST no. C17-200, set-booster insert). The Commander 2017 inclusion in a purpose-built vampire-tribal shell explains the heavy EDH vampire commander representation in its EDHREC data.
sources: Scryfall: all prints search for Tithe Drinker

trivia_bullets.text: Tithe Drinker is the only card in Slawomir Maniak's entire illustrated catalog (222+ cards by Gatherer count) to carry an Orzhov watermark. His other vampire work is all Innistrad-plane: Falkenrath Noble (Innistrad), Screeching Bat // Stalking Vampire (Innistrad), Bloodflow Connoisseur (Avacyn Restored). No Hipsters of the Coast profile or artist statement for Maniak was found via search.

Dragon's Maze is widely considered one of the weakest sets in the game's history; community discourse cites cramming all ten Return to Ravnica guild mechanics into a small set, with prerelease attendance reportedly down ~50% from Gatecrash. Tithe Drinker was a notable exception: the MTGSalvation Pauper set review called it 'the best [common] in this set' and a commenter noted it is 'probably the best common of the set.' (Community sentiment.)
sources: MTGSalvation: Dragon's Maze — Major Flop at the Finish Line
MTGSalvation: Pauper Set Review Dragon's Maze

trivia_bullets.text: The Dragon's Maze block narrative centers on the Implicit Maze, a system of leylines through all ten guild districts, designed by Azorius parun Azor as a Guildpact fail-safe. Each guild sent one champion; the Orzhov representative was Teysa Karlov (Human Advisor, proxy for Obzedat). The unnamed vampire depicted on Tithe Drinker is not identified with Teysa in any canon source — the figure is a generic syndic. Jace Beleren became the Living Guildpact by connecting all ten runners' minds at the maze's conclusion.
sources: MTG Wiki: Implicit Maze (via WebSearch summary)

related_cards.name: Syndic of Tithes
related_cards.note: Directly-compared WB extort predecessor; Tithe Drinker is widely framed as its lifelink-bearing successor.

related_cards.name: Basilica Screecher
related_cards.note: Another common Orzhov extort creature from Return to Ravnica block; co-staple of WB extort Pauper builds.

related_cards.name: Cruel Celebrant
related_cards.note: Top Orzhov-vampire EDHREC synergy partner; on-death drain stacks with Tithe Drinker lifelink/extort plan.

related_cards.name: Sanguine Bond
related_cards.note: EDHREC 53% inclusion synergy in vampire-tribal shells that play Tithe Drinker.

related_cards.name: Exquisite Blood
related_cards.note: EDHREC 37% inclusion synergy; pairs with lifelink and extort drain in EDH.

related_cards.name: Blind Obedience
related_cards.note: Mono-white Extort card whose Commander color-identity ruling became the defining precedent for all Extort cards.

related_cards.name: Teysa Karlov
related_cards.note: Named Orzhov guild champion in the Dragon's Maze narrative; top EDH commander for the vampire-aristocrat style Tithe Drinker inhabits.
